connector_workflow.output: Upload sessions + presigned PUT targets for all 8 provided input assets into CC storage
connector_workflow.note: [C] Bring every supplied asset (2 bottle concepts, label concept, hero shot, SKU-line reference render, brand brief PDF, the USER-AUTHORED Illustrator data-merge template carrying real Variables, and the dieline label template) into CC storage so later tools can reference them by URN. Pairs with asset_get_presigned_urls / asset_finalize_file_upload. Headless-confirmed.

connector_workflow.output: 8 CC asset URNs (urn:bottle_A, urn:bottle_B, urn:label, urn:hero, urn:sku_line, urn:brief, urn:merge_ai, urn:template)
connector_workflow.note: [C] Finalizes the uploads opened in step 1 and returns stable URNs reused across the whole workflow. The agent must keep this URN map and not re-upload. Every downstream step references these URNs, not raw filenames.

connector_workflow.output: Stock results: matte-black slate texture + brushed-silver surface candidates for the brand world
connector_workflow.note: [C] Pull two on-brand texture backgrounds (matte charcoal slate, brushed metal) that the moodboard and box dieline will sit on. Query terms derived from the brief's visual direction (urn:brief). Adobe Stock scope, headless-confirmed.

connector_workflow.output: 2 licensed Stock textures downloaded into CC storage → urn:tex_charcoal, urn:tex_metal
connector_workflow.note: [C] Licenses the two chosen textures BEFORE any editing/use so they are legally usable in the deliverables, returning full-res presigned URLs. Feeds the moodboard (step 5) and the box dieline (step 16). License-before-edit order respected.

connector_workflow.output: Firefly Board moodboard 'BARDE — Brand World v1' (deep-link) assembling both bottle concepts, the label concept, the hero shot, the 6-SKU line reference render, and the 2 licensed textures
connector_workflow.note: [C] MUST-EXERCISE. Concept board that fixes the matte-black/deep-red direction. Assembles already-produced asset URNs (it does not itself generate art — honest use). Now also consumes urn:sku_line as the line-family reference tile, giving that asset a real downstream consumer. This is the identity anchor referenced by every downstream visual.

connector_workflow.output: Type system: a bold condensed grotesque for the wordmark + a humanist sans for body, with rationale
connector_workflow.note: [C] Derives the typography system from the brief ('strong wordmark', minimal/bold) and the locked moodboard mood (step 5). Font choices are reused in the label master (step 13), box (step 16), routine card (step 17) and style guide (step 18). Headless-confirmed.

connector_workflow.output: Clean SVG wordmark/logomark vector → urn:logo_svg
connector_workflow.note: [C] Raster → clean SVG. Turns the supplied raster label/wordmark concept (urn:label from the step-2 URN map) into a crisp print-ready editable vector logo (brief demands editable source + dielines). The SVG is placed into the label master, box, card, and guide. inputs_from now correctly references the step-2 URN, not the raw filename.

connector_workflow.output: Selection mask isolating the seamless backdrop region around the bottle (background, not the bottle) on the clean hero shot → mask_bg
connector_workflow.note: [C] Selects the studio backdrop AROUND the bottle so a uniform brand-charcoal can be flood-filled behind it (a legitimate solid-color use). NOTE: the previous spec used this to mark 'water droplets + lamp reflection' for removal — that is AI object-removal, which is NOT AVAILABLE (A6 [X]). The hero input is now generated already-clean (no distractions to remove), so the select is repurposed to a real solid-fill target. inputs_from references the step-2 URN.

connector_workflow.output: Hero with the masked backdrop flood-filled to an exact brand-charcoal RGB (#1A1A1A) behind the intact bottle → hero_charcoal.png
connector_workflow.note: [C] CORRECTED USE. image_fill_area is SOLID-COLOR fill of a selection ONLY — not generative, not object-removal, not a compositor. Here it flood-fills the step-8 background mask with a single brand-charcoal RGB so the hero sits on the locked brand world. No object is removed and no content is generated. (Previous spec misused this as 'generative fill removes droplets+lamp' — fixed.)

connector_workflow.output: Tone-balanced hero on brand-charcoal → hero_toned.png
connector_workflow.note: [C] Normalizes exposure/contrast on the charcoal-backed hero so the upcoming generative expand blends seamlessly and the banner reads premium. Headless-confirmed tonal tool.

connector_workflow.output: Hero outpainted to a 2400×1000 DTC banner canvas with matte-charcoal negative space → hero_banner.png
connector_workflow.note: [C] MUST-EXERCISE — the ONLY genuinely generative tool (AI outpaint / canvas extend). Extends the single toned product shot into a wide ecommerce/social banner, matching the moodboard's charcoal world (step 5). The only generative step in the whole chain, per the corrected sheet. Reused in the social crops and handoff.

connector_workflow.output: Two crops of the banner: 1080×1080 IG square + 1080×1920 story → hero_ig.png, hero_story.png
connector_workflow.note: [C] Subject-aware crops of the expanded 2400×1000 banner give the 'social-media friendly' assets the brief calls for, without re-shooting. Chains directly off step 11's wide canvas. Two crops produced from one call's resize/crop ops on the same source.

connector_workflow.output: Master 50ml roll-on sleeve label artwork (2mm bleed + dieline + 3mm safety) as a print-ready vector → label_master.svg/pdf
connector_workflow.note: [C] Renders the spec-exact label master: places the vectorized logo (step 7 → urn:logo_svg) and chosen fonts (step 6) onto the supplied dieline template (urn:template). Exports the genuine .ai artwork. 2mm bleed, 3mm interior safety — consistent everywhere. This is the visual reference the data-merge template (step 14) mirrors.

connector_workflow.output: 6 per-SKU label variants (variant_name / concern / size / accent swapped per row) as print-ready vector pages → labels_merged/01-06.pdf
connector_workflow.note: [T] AUTHORED-TEMPLATE. Illustrator Variables data-merge: requires the USER-AUTHORED .ai (urn:merge_ai) carrying REAL Variables that bind to the 6-row CSV. Rolls the master label design across the full 6-SKU line. A PDF-converted .ai with literal text would NOT bind — that is why the merge template is a supplied authored asset, not the step-13 render. Tagged [T] because it needs the user's real Variable-bearing source.

connector_workflow.output: Patch-pouch label artwork (small sachet face, same logo+type system, accent bar) as print-ready vector → patch_pouch.svg/pdf
connector_workflow.note: [C] Covers the REAL dropped deliverable: the patch pouch named in the source Upwork brief. Reuses the same logo (urn:logo_svg) + font system (step 6) on a pouch-format face so brand consistency holds across label, pouch, box, card, guide. Closes the coverage gap the verdict raised.

connector_workflow.output: Outer product box dieline artwork (front/back/sides) referencing the matte-charcoal texture → box_dieline.svg/pdf
connector_workflow.note: [C] Generates the outer carton with the same logo+type system, styled to the licensed charcoal texture (step 4). Mirrors the label system for brand consistency. Genuine .ai → PDF/SVG render.

connector_workflow.output: A6 2-up routine/instruction card → routine_card.pdf
connector_workflow.note: [C] Splits the old combined step: this call renders ONLY the routine/instruction card (A6, 2-up) from a genuine .indd. Uses palette from moodboard (step 5), type from step 6, logo from step 7. Style guide is a separate render (step 18).

connector_workflow.output: 1-page brand style-guide sheet (palette hex+CMYK values, type system, logo lockups) → styleguide.pdf
connector_workflow.note: [C] Second of the two split layout renders: the single-sheet style guide. Lists palette (hex + CMYK swatch values authored in the layout), type system, and logo lockups. CMYK values are authored content in the sheet, not a claim of print-engine color conversion. Genuine .indd → PDF.

connector_workflow.output: The single-page label master converted PDF→.indd editable source package → label_master_source.indd (ZIP)
connector_workflow.note: [C] CORRECTED USE. document_convert_pdf does PDF→genuine .indd (ZIP); it does NOT merge several files into one multipage PDF (A6/A2). Used here only to hand the client an editable .indd source of the single label master (brief: 'editable source files'). The 6-page label booklet collation is done LOCALLY in step 20, NOT here.

connector_workflow.output: Single print-layout PDF booklet of all 6 merged SKU labels with dielines/bleed retained → sku_labels.pdf
connector_workflow.note: [L] LOCAL glue. Concatenate the 6 per-SKU merged label pages (step 14) into one multipage print-layout PDF (e.g. via a PDF toolkit), preserving each page's 2mm bleed and dieline marks. Done off-connector because document_convert_pdf cannot merge multiple files into one PDF. Output is a print-layout PDF with dielines/bleed intact — NOT asserted as verified PDF/X or hardware CMYK-proofed.

connector_workflow.output: All final deliverables uploaded into a single 'BARDE_Handoff' CC folder
connector_workflow.note: [C] Uploads every generated artifact (charcoal hero, banner, social crops, label master, patch pouch, box dieline, routine card, style guide, editable .indd source, 6-SKU booklet PDF) into one organized handoff folder for the client.

connector_workflow.output: Contact-sheet previews + shareable links for the full handoff package
connector_workflow.note: [C] Renders previews of the packaged deliverables so the annotator/client can review the complete brand+packaging system at a glance. Closes the loop on the handoff.

corrections_applied: TOOL MISUSE (object removal) FIXED: step 9 image_fill_area was used as a 'generative fill' that 'removes water droplets + lamp reflection'. AI object/element removal is A6 [X] NOT AVAILABLE, and image_fill_area is SOLID-COLOR fill only. Re-grounded the whole hero chain: the hero input is now generated already-clean (no distractions), step 8 image_select_by_prompt selects the backdrop, and step 9 flood-fills that selection with an exact brand-charcoal RGB (#1A1A1A) — a legitimate solid-color use. Removed every 'generatively removed' / 'generative fill' claim.
Confirmed image_generative_expand (step 11) is the ONLY generative step and left it intact and correctly tagged [C].

DATA-MERGE re-grounded as [T]: document_merge_data_vector (step 14) now consumes a USER-AUTHORED Illustrator template carrying REAL Variables (new input sku_data_vector_ai → urn:merge_ai) bound to the CSV rows (urn:csv_rows), instead of binding to the step-13 rendered master (a literal-text render does NOT bind). Tagged exec_mode [T].

document_convert_pdf MERGE misuse FIXED: old step 15 used document_convert_pdf to 'collate 6 merged labels into one PDF' — it cannot merge files. Re-tasked step 19 to its real capability (PDF→editable .indd source for the label master) and moved the 6-page booklet collation to a new LOCAL step 20 (exec_mode [L]).

CMYK / PDF-X OVERREACH SOFTENED: dropped 'press-ready CMYK PDF/X-style' assertions. Step 20 + sku_labels_pdf now read 'print-layout PDF with dielines/bleed retained'; CMYK values in the style guide are authored swatch content, not a claim of color-engine conversion. Rubric hooks regrade dieline/bleed integrity + 6-page row correctness instead of asserting verified CMYK/PDF-X.
BLEED CONTRADICTION FIXED: difficulty_rationale now says '2mm bleed, 3mm safety margin', matching full_brief, the label template gen_prompt, step 13, and the label_master output everywhere.

ORPHANED INPUT FIXED: sku_line_render (urn:sku_line) now has a real downstream consumer — it is assembled into the Firefly moodboard (step 5) as the line-family reference tile. used_in_steps updated to [1,2,5].

URN BOOKKEEPING DRIFT FIXED: steps 7 (image_vectorize) and 8 (image_select_by_prompt) now reference the step-2 URNs (urn:label, urn:hero) instead of raw asset filenames, consistent with the 'reuse the URN map, do not re-upload' premise.
VERTICAL MISLABEL FIXED: changed from 'Wedding & Stationery' to 'Beauty & Grooming / Brand Identity & Packaging'.
PATCH POUCH COVERAGE GAP CLOSED: added step 15 document_render_vector that renders the patch-pouch artwork reusing the logo+type system, covering the real named deliverable from the source brief.
STEP 17 SPLIT: the single document_render_layout that emitted both routine_card.pdf and styleguide.pdf is split into two renders (step 17 = routine card, step 18 = style guide).

fill_text / Express [W] track: not used anywhere (no placeholder-text template fills) — nothing to demote. No media_enhance_speech / video_resize / video_create_quick_cut steps exist (no audio/video in this brief), so no music-enhance or trim misuse to fix.

Every step now carries exec_mode ∈ {C,W,A,T,L}; all headless-confirmed connector calls are [C], the Illustrator data-merge is [T], and the booklet collation is [L]. No A6 [X] capability appears in any step.
Lengthened to 22 connector/local calls (≥12 connector calls), preserving the continuous output→input chain across asset/stock/storage, photo-raster, and document/layout groups.
difficulty_rationale: This is hard because it is a true identity-to-packaging pipeline with 22 chained steps spanning four capability groups (asset/stock/storage, photo-raster, document/layout, plus the Firefly moodboard) and demands heavy long-horizon state tracking. The agent must: (1) upload 8 assets and maintain a URN map it reuses across ~12 later steps without re-uploading; (2) build a moodboard that locks a visual direction every downstream step must honor (matte charcoal + deep red, derived from the brief, not invented); (3) run a raster chain where each output is the next input — select backdrop → solid-color flood-fill to brand charcoal → auto-tone → generative-expand into a 2400×1000 banner → 1080×1080 and 1080×1920 social crops — understanding that the ONLY generative step allowed is image_generative_expand (outpaint) and that object removal / generative fill are simply not available, so the hero must arrive clean and image_fill_area is used strictly as a solid-color fill; (4) construct the parametric vector label master, then execute a data-merge across a 6-row SKU CSV using a USER-AUTHORED Illustrator template that carries real Variables (a literal-text render would not bind), keeping per-record fields and accent colors correct, then collate the 6 pages to one print-layout booklet LOCALLY because document_convert_pdf cannot merge files; (5) hold exact-spec pressure throughout — 50ml sleeve with 2mm bleed, 3mm safety margin, the supplied dieline, 2400×1000 banner, 1080×1080 and 1080×1920 crops, A6 card, single-sheet guide — while reusing the same logo SVG and font system across label, patch pouch, box, card, and guide for brand consistency. The big trap is reaching for a tool that does not exist (generative object removal of the droplets/lamp, or PDF-merge via document_convert_pdf), or firing the must-exercise tools on the wrong upstream artifact, which silently breaks the brand-consistency rubric the human annotator scores.
full_brief:
BARDE Grooming (working name, shared only with the awarded freelancer) is launching a premium men's grooming/skincare brand. The flagship is a 50ml post-shave / post-haircut roll-on serum aimed at men prone to razor bumps, ingrown hairs, irritation, and dark marks around the neck and beard line. The brand must read premium, masculine, modern, barber-led and performance-focused — built for both DTC ecommerce and barbershop retail — and must NOT look generic, overly clinical, feminine, or like a cheap private-label product. Visual direction: matte black / deep charcoal packaging with cream, white, silver, or deep-red accents; a minimal-but-bold layout; a strong wordmark; and a social-media-friendly, lifestyle-driven feel (reference point for boldness only, not to copy: 'Sleep or Die').

You are hired for the Full Brand Package. Deliver: a logo/wordmark, color palette, typography system, and a basic brand style guide; the 50ml roll-on sleeve/label design; an outer product box design; a patch pouch design; a routine/instruction card; product mockups and social assets; and print-ready files with dielines plus editable source files. Enriched for this benchmark into a long-horizon pipeline, the engagement also requires: (a) a Firefly moodboard that locks the brand world from the supplied bottle/label concepts, the 6-SKU line reference, and licensed textures; (b) a hero product shot placed on the locked brand-charcoal world via a solid-color backdrop fill and tonal balance; (c) that hero outpainted into a wide ecommerce/social banner and cropped to platform sizes; and (d) the locked label design rolled across the brand's full six-SKU line via an Illustrator Variables data-merge driven by a user-authored template, then collated locally to one print-layout booklet.

Constraints and specs: 50ml roll-on shrink-sleeve/label must carry a 2mm bleed and the supplied dieline, keep critical content >=3mm inside trim (3mm safety margin), and be delivered as print-layout artwork with dielines/bleed retained plus editable source. SKU line = 6 records driven by the provided CSV (variant name, concern, strength, size, accent color). Banner = 2400x1000 px; social crops = 1080x1080 (IG square) and 1080x1920 (story). Routine card = A6, 2-up. Style guide = single sheet with palette (hex + CMYK swatch values), type system, and logo lockups. The same vectorized logo and font system must appear consistently on the label, patch pouch, box, card, and guide — brand consistency is explicitly graded. IMPORTANT CAPABILITY NOTES (grounded to the connector): the hero's distractions are NOT removed by an AI tool (object/generative removal is not available) — the supplied hero is already clean and image_fill_area is used only for a solid-color backdrop fill; the SKU rollout uses a USER-AUTHORED Illustrator data-merge template with real Variables; the 6-page label booklet is assembled with a local PDF step because the connector cannot merge files into one PDF; CMYK is authored as swatch values, not a verified color-engine conversion. Hand off all artifacts in one organized CC folder with previews.
grounding_note: REAL (verbatim from the Upwork brief in db_all.json): launching a new men's grooming/skincare brand needing a full brand identity + packaging system; flagship is a 50ml post-shave/post-haircut roll-on serum for razor bumps, ingrown hairs, irritation and dark marks on neck/beard line; brand must feel premium, masculine, modern, barber-led, performance-focused, for DTC ecommerce + barbershop retail; must NOT look generic/clinical/feminine/cheap private-label; matte black / deep charcoal packaging with cream/white/silver/deep-red accents, minimal-but-bold layout, strong wordmark, social-media-friendly; 'Sleep or Die' cited only as a boldness reference (not to copy). REAL named deliverables: logo/wordmark, colour palette, typography system, basic brand style guide, 50ml roll-on sleeve/label design, outer product box design, patch pouch design, routine/instruction card design, product mockups, print-ready files with dielines, editable source files. Tools listed: InDesign, Illustrator. Budget: Fixed price, Intermediate. ENRICHED (implied by the brief, added to make it long-horizon and to exercise the tools HONESTLY against the corrected capability sheet): the Firefly moodboard step; placing the hero on the locked brand-charcoal world via image_select_by_prompt + image_fill_area SOLID-COLOR backdrop fill (NOT object removal — AI object/generative removal is not available, so the hero ships clean); the image_generative_expand into a 2400x1000 banner (the only generative step) and the 1080x1080/1080x1920 social crops; the 6-SKU rollout via a USER-AUTHORED Illustrator Variables template (document_merge_data_vector [T]) bound to a provided CSV; local assembly of the 6 merged pages into one booklet PDF (the connector cannot merge files); the Adobe Stock charcoal/metal textures; exact pixel/A6 specs; and the working brand name 'BARDE' (the real client withholds the name until shortlisting, so a placeholder is used). The patch pouch — a REAL named deliverable previously dropped — is now exercised via a document_render_vector step reusing the logo+type system. The brief asks for a brand 'system' and 'product mockups' and SKU-friendly identity but does not literally enumerate six SKUs — the CSV-driven line rollout is an honest extension of 'full brand/packaging system'.

inputs.name: bottle_concept_A
inputs.gen_prompt: Studio product render of a 50ml matte-black roll-on serum bottle for a premium men's grooming brand, deep charcoal soft-touch finish, brushed-silver roller cap, blank label area, shot on a dark charcoal seamless background with a single soft key light and subtle rim light, realistic micro-scratches and fingerprint-resistant matte texture on the plastic, true-to-life specular highlights, shallow depth of field, premium e-commerce product photography, no text on label, photorealistic not CGI-glossy
inputs.realism_notes: Must look like a real photographed bottle: visible matte surface microtexture, believable soft shadow, no plastic-perfect CGI sheen, label area genuinely blank for later artwork.

inputs.name: bottle_concept_B
inputs.gen_prompt: Alternate concept render of the same 50ml men's grooming roll-on serum, this time with a deep oxblood-red accent ring at the cap seam and cream-colored label zone, three-quarter angle on a brushed-metal surface, dramatic single-source barbershop lighting, realistic reflections in the metal, fine dust and a faint fingerprint smudge for authenticity, premium masculine product still life, photoreal
inputs.realism_notes: Second direction so the moodboard has a real A/B choice; keep accent placement plausible for a shrink sleeve; authentic reflections and minor real-world imperfections.

inputs.name: label_concept
inputs.gen_prompt: Flat 2D wordmark/label concept for a bold premium men's grooming brand named 'BARDE', strong condensed grotesque all-caps wordmark in cream on matte black, a small geometric barber-blade monogram mark above it, deep-red underline accent, minimal layout, crisp high-contrast edges suitable for vectorization, centered, transparent-safe flat artwork, no photographic background
inputs.realism_notes: Text-bearing/logo art so use the text-capable model; edges must be clean enough that image_vectorize produces a usable SVG; legible 'BARDE' wordmark and a simple mark.

inputs.name: hero_product_shot
inputs.gen_prompt: Clean hero e-commerce photograph of the finished 50ml matte-black BARDE roll-on serum standing on wet dark slate, single dramatic side light, premium masculine mood, NO water droplets and NO visible reflected lamp/softbox in the cap — a pristine, distraction-free product shot; photoreal, sharp, shallow depth of field, real matte surface microtexture, believable soft shadow, not CGI-glossy
inputs.realism_notes: CORRECTED: the hero is now generated already-clean (no droplets, no lamp reflection) because AI object removal is not available on the connector. image_select_by_prompt + image_fill_area are used only for a SOLID-COLOR backdrop fill behind the intact bottle, not for removing anything from the bottle itself.

inputs.name: sku_line_render
inputs.gen_prompt: Group product photograph of six matte-black men's grooming bottles of slightly different sizes/variants lined up on charcoal, consistent brand finish, varied accent-ring colors (red, silver, cream, steel-blue, olive, bronze), realistic studio lighting and soft shadows, premium catalog row shot, photoreal, blank labels
inputs.realism_notes: Reference for the 6-SKU family; now an actual consumed asset — it is assembled into the Firefly moodboard (step 5) as the line-family reference tile so the data-merge output can be sanity-checked against a plausible line. Blank labels so merged artwork is the star.

inputs.name: brand_brief_pdf
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a 1-page brand brief PDF for 'BARDE' premium men's grooming brand: positioning (premium, masculine, barber-led, performance-focused, DTC + barbershop), flagship 50ml post-shave roll-on serum for razor bumps/ingrowns/irritation/dark marks, visual direction (matte black/charcoal, cream/white/silver/deep-red accents, minimal-but-bold, strong wordmark, social-friendly), and a deliverables checklist (logo, palette, type, style guide, 50ml label, box, patch pouch, routine card, mockups, print-ready files + editable source)
inputs.realism_notes: Plausible client-facing brief; drives stock search terms (step 3) and font rationale (step 6).

inputs.name: sku_data_csv
inputs.gen_prompt: Produce a 6-row CSV for a men's grooming line with columns: sku_code, variant_name, concern, size_ml, strength, accent_hex, accent_name. Rows like: BRD-01,Post-Shave Roll-On,Razor bumps,50,Daily,#8E1B1B,Oxblood; BRD-02,Beard-Line Serum,Ingrown hairs,30,Intensive,#C0C0C0,Silver; plus four more plausible variants with distinct accent_hex values
inputs.realism_notes: Real-looking merge data; exactly six rows; each accent_hex must be a valid hex so per-SKU accent recoloring is checkable in the merge output. This CSV is the data source bound to the user-authored Illustrator Variables template in step 14.

inputs.name: sku_data_vector_ai
inputs.gen_model: document_merge_data_vector requires a USER-AUTHORED Illustrator (.ai) source — authored in Illustrator with Variables, not model-generated art
inputs.gen_prompt: USER-AUTHORED Adobe Illustrator (.ai) label artboard for the 50ml roll-on sleeve, built to the supplied dieline (2mm bleed, 3mm safety) and wired with named Illustrator VARIABLES bound to the CSV columns: a text variable for VARIANT_NAME, CONCERN, SIZE_ML, STRENGTH; and a fill/object variable for the ACCENT_BAR color (accent_hex). The wordmark and a placeholder body type are placed as static art. This is a genuine Variables-bearing source so document_merge_data_vector can bind one page per CSV row.
inputs.realism_notes: CRITICAL for [T] correctness: document_merge_data_vector only binds against a real user-authored .ai with actual Variables; a literal-text render (e.g. the step-13 output) does NOT bind. This authored template is supplied as input and uploaded in steps 1-2 as urn:merge_ai, then consumed in step 14 alongside the CSV rows.

inputs.name: label_template_vector
inputs.gen_prompt: A clean 50ml roll-on shrink-sleeve label dieline template as flat vector artwork: rectangular sleeve panel with marked 2mm bleed border, a dashed dieline/trim edge, a 3mm interior safety margin, labeled placeholder zones for WORDMARK, VARIANT NAME, CONCERN, SIZE 50ml, BATCH/BARCODE, and an ACCENT BAR region; neutral gray placeholders, no real brand content, print-spec accurate
inputs.realism_notes: Must encode real print structure (2mm bleed / trim / 3mm safety + named fields) so document_render_vector (step 13), the patch pouch (step 15), and the box (step 16) have a genuine dieline to build on. Distinct from sku_data_vector_ai: this is the dieline reference; that is the Variables-wired merge source.

iterative_chaining_note: The workflow is a continuous output-to-input chain, not parallel one-offs. Storage: steps 1-2 turn the 8 supplied assets into a URN map reused in steps 5, 7, 8, 13, 14, 15, 16. Stock: step 3's search results feed step 4's licensing, whose two licensed textures feed the moodboard (5) and the box dieline (16). Identity: step 5's Firefly moodboard (now also consuming urn:sku_line as the line-family tile) locks the visual world consumed by font_recommend (6), the generative expand (11), and the layout deliverables (17, 18). Logo: label_concept → image_vectorize (7, reading urn:label) → urn:logo_svg placed into the label master (13), patch pouch (15), box (16), card (17), guide (18). CORRECTED raster chain: hero (already clean) → image_select_by_prompt selects the BACKDROP (8, reading urn:hero) → image_fill_area flood-fills that selection with a single brand-charcoal RGB (9) — a solid-color fill, NOT object removal/compositing → image_apply_auto_tone (10) → image_generative_expand outpaints the toned hero into a 2400x1000 banner (11, the ONLY generative step) → image_crop_and_resize produces the 1080 square/story crops (12). Data-merge core: step 13 document_render_vector builds the label master from urn:logo_svg + fonts + urn:template; step 14 document_merge_data_vector [T] binds the USER-AUTHORED Variables template (urn:merge_ai) to the 6-row CSV → 6 per-SKU pages; step 20 [L] locally concatenates those 6 pages into one print-layout booklet (the connector cannot merge files). Editable source: step 19 document_convert_pdf turns the single label-master PDF into an editable .indd package (its real capability), NOT a multi-file merge. Coverage: step 15 adds the patch-pouch render. Handoff: steps 9, 11, 12, 13, 15, 16, 17, 18, 19, 20 all feed asset_add_file into one folder (21), which feeds asset_inline_preview (22). The must-exercise junctions: create_firefly_board (5) gates the brand world, the corrected solid-fill hero (9) + generative-expand (11) gate the social set, and the [T] data-merge (14) gates the SKU-line packaging output.
one_line_ask: Deliver a complete BARDE men's-grooming brand and packaging system — moodboard, logo/type, a hero shot placed on the brand-charcoal world and outpainted into social banners, a 50ml roll-on label rolled across the full 6-SKU line via an authored Illustrator data-merge, plus box, patch pouch, routine card and style guide — as organized, editable, print-layout CC assets.

outputs.name: brand_moodboard
outputs.spec: Firefly Board deep-link assembling 2 bottle concepts + label concept + hero + 6-SKU line reference + 2 licensed textures; locks matte-charcoal/deep-red direction
outputs.rubric_hook: Does the board cohere to the brief's visual direction (matte black/charcoal, cream/silver/deep-red), include the supplied concepts and the line reference, and read premium/masculine rather than generic?

outputs.name: logo_vector
outputs.spec: Clean SVG wordmark + monogram derived from label_concept; print-ready, scalable, editable paths
outputs.rubric_hook: Is the vector crisp (no raster fringing), is the 'BARDE' wordmark legible at small sizes, and does it match the moodboard mark?

outputs.name: hero_brandworld
outputs.spec: Hero product shot with the studio backdrop solid-color-filled to brand charcoal (#1A1A1A) behind the intact bottle; auto-toned; bottle untouched; same resolution as source
outputs.rubric_hook: Is the backdrop a clean uniform brand-charcoal fill with the bottle fully intact (no object removed, no halo on the bottle edge), and is exposure balanced? (Note: no AI object removal is claimed — the hero was supplied clean.)

outputs.name: hero_banner
outputs.spec: 2400x1000 px outpainted ecommerce/social banner, charcoal negative space consistent with the moodboard, bottle subject intact
outputs.rubric_hook: Is the expand seamless (no seam line, consistent lighting/color), correct 2400x1000 dimensions, and does the added canvas match the brand world?

outputs.name: social_crops
outputs.spec: 1080x1080 IG square + 1080x1920 story crops of the banner, subject-aware (bottle not cut off)
outputs.rubric_hook: Exact dimensions; is the bottle well-composed in both ratios; consistent with the banner?

outputs.name: label_master
outputs.spec: 50ml roll-on sleeve master: logo + type placed on supplied dieline, 2mm bleed, 3mm safety margin, accent bar
outputs.rubric_hook: Are bleed (2mm) / trim / safety (3mm) respected, fields correctly placed, and does it use the locked logo + recommended fonts?

outputs.name: sku_labels_pdf
outputs.spec: Single print-layout PDF, 6 pages (one per CSV SKU), variant name/concern/size/accent swapped per row from a user-authored Illustrator Variables merge, dielines/bleed retained on every page; assembled locally
outputs.rubric_hook: Do all 6 labels match their CSV rows (correct variant text + accent_hex), and are dielines + 2mm bleed intact on every page? (Graded on dieline/bleed integrity and 6-page row correctness — NOT on verified PDF/X or hardware-CMYK proofing, which the connector does not guarantee.)

outputs.name: patch_pouch
outputs.spec: Patch pouch / sachet face artwork on the brand world, same logo+type system, accent bar; print-layout with dieline
outputs.rubric_hook: Is the pouch brand-consistent with the labels (same logo/fonts/palette) and is the dieline structurally valid? (Covers the previously-dropped real deliverable.)

outputs.name: box_dieline
outputs.spec: Outer carton dieline (front/back/sides) styled to the licensed charcoal texture, same logo+type system
outputs.rubric_hook: Is the box brand-consistent with the labels (same logo/fonts/palette) and is the dieline structurally valid?

outputs.name: routine_card
outputs.rubric_hook: Does the routine card match the brand system (logo/fonts/palette) and is it A6, 2-up?

outputs.name: style_guide
outputs.spec: 1-page style guide (palette hex + CMYK swatch values, type system, logo lockups)
outputs.rubric_hook: Does the style guide list accurate palette (hex + CMYK swatch values authored in the sheet) + type system + logo lockups? (CMYK values are authored content, not a claim of engine color conversion.)

outputs.name: editable_source
outputs.spec: Editable .indd source package of the label master, produced by document_convert_pdf (PDF→.indd)
outputs.rubric_hook: Is an editable source file delivered for the label master, satisfying the brief's 'editable source files' requirement?

outputs.name: handoff_package
outputs.spec: One CC folder containing all deliverables with inline preview contact sheet + shareable links
outputs.rubric_hook: Is every deliverable present, organized, and previewable in a single handoff?

title: Brand Identity + Packaging Designer for Premium Men's Skincare/Grooming Brand

residual_issues: Step 12: a single image_crop_and_resize call is claimed to emit TWO distinct named outputs (1080x1080 hero_ig.png AND 1080x1920 hero_story.png). image_crop_and_resize is geometry on one source producing one result per call; two different target crops realistically require two separate calls. This conflates two operations into one step and slightly under-counts the true call total. Not a capability violation (both crops are valid uses of the tool), but the 'one call -> two crops' framing is a stretch. Splitting into 12a/12b would make it honest.

Step 16 references the licensed charcoal RASTER texture (step 4) inside a document_render_vector (.ai) render. This is feasible only as a placed raster inside the .ai artboard, not as a vector element; the spec wording 'styled to the licensed charcoal texture' is fine but graders should confirm it is a placed-image reference, not a claim that the texture becomes vector. Cosmetic/wording-level, no tool misuse.

Minor: create_firefly_board (step 5) consumes uploaded CC asset URNs (bottle concepts, hero, label, sku_line) plus licensed stock. The sheet frames honest use as 'present a board of assets you already produced'; these are user-supplied inputs rather than agent-produced generations. The spec explicitly states the board 'does not itself generate art', so this is within bounds, but it leans on the 'CC assets' allowance rather than 'Firefly generations'. Acceptable, noted for completeness.
